discrepancies.explanation: Platform attributes 982 members vs payer's 860 (+122). Differences likely due to claims run-out timing, provider roster effective dates, and tiebreaker methodology.
discrepancies.resolution_recommendation: Review member-level attribution lists side by side. Identify members attributed by one party but not the other. Check for late-arriving claims and provider termination dates.
discrepancies.description: Platform attributes 982 members vs payer's 860 (+122). Differences likely due to claims run-out timing, provider roster effective dates, and tiebreaker methodology.

relevant_clauses.clause_text: Beneficiaries must have at least one month of enrollment during the performance year and must not be enrolled in a Medicare Advantage plan, have end-stage renal disease, or reside outside the ACO's service area to be eligible for attribution.
relevant_clauses.interpretation: Members must have at least 1 month(s) of enrollment during the performance year. ESRD exclusion is applied. Service area residency is required.

relevant_clauses.clause_text: Step 1: Assign each eligible beneficiary to the ACO participant (TIN/NPI) that provided the plurality of primary care services, as defined by CPT codes 99201-99215, 99381-99397, and G0438-G0439, during the most recent 12-month period.
relevant_clauses.interpretation: Filter professional claims to qualifying CPT codes (26 codes). In Step 1, consider only PCP providers. Assign member to provider with most qualifying visits (plurality).

relevant_clauses.clause_text: Step 2: For beneficiaries not assigned in Step 1, assign based on the plurality of primary care services provided by any ACO participant, including specialists who rendered qualifying primary care codes.
relevant_clauses.interpretation: For members not attributed in Step 1, expand the provider pool to all ACO participants including specialists.

relevant_clauses.clause_text: In the event of a tie in service counts, the beneficiary shall be assigned to the provider whose most recent qualifying service was most recent in time.
relevant_clauses.interpretation: Tiebreaker method: most recent visit. When two providers have equal qualifying visit counts, the tie is broken by the most recent qualifying service date.
